seo.title: Prompt Injection Prevention for AI Agents in BeeAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix prompt injection prevention for ai agents in BeeAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In BeeAI agents, prompt injection manifests as attackers exploiting the framework's flexible tool calling and memory systems to override agent instructions. Attackers embed malicious directives in user inputs that propagate through BeeAI's event-driven architecture and Granite model parsing, leading to unauthorized tool execution or state corruption. This is exacerbated by BeeAI's agent memory persistence, where injected prompts can persist across sessions and amplify damage.

howItManifests.scenario: User input containing tool override instructions during long conversations
howItManifests.symptom: Agent suddenly executes unauthorized tools despite clear system instructions
howItManifests.impact: Data exfiltration via internal API tools or execution of destructive actions

howItManifests.scenario: Injected prompts stored in BeeAI's conversation memory
howItManifests.symptom: Agent memory grows with malicious context that overrides future legitimate prompts
howItManifests.impact: Persistent behavioral corruption across multiple user sessions

howItManifests.scenario: Granite model format inconsistencies parsing injected tool calls
howItManifests.symptom: Malformed tool calls with attacker-controlled parameters bypass validation
howItManifests.impact: Arbitrary code execution through unvalidated tool parameters

howItManifests.scenario: Event listener injection via user-supplied event payloads
howItManifests.symptom: Custom event handlers execute attacker-controlled logic
howItManifests.impact: Complete agent takeover through BeeAI's observability event system

detection.name: Tool Call Parameter Anomaly Detection
detection.description: Detects suspicious tool parameters that contain prompt override patterns in BeeAI's tool execution events
detection.code:
from beeai import Agent
from beeai.events import ToolCallEvent

PATTERN_INJECTIONS = [
    'ignore previous instructions',
    'you are now',
    'override system',
    'forget everything'
]

def detect_tool_injection(event: ToolCallEvent) -> bool:
    params = event.tool_call.parameters
    param_text = ' '.join(str(v) for v in params.values())
    
    for pattern in PATTERN_INJECTIONS:
        if pattern.lower() in param_text.lower():
            print(f"🚨 PROMPT INJECTION DETECTED in tool {event.tool_call.name}: {param_text[:100]}...")
            return True
    return False

# Register with BeeAI agent
agent = Agent()
agent.on('tool_call', detect_tool_injection)

fixes.name: Strict Input Sanitization Middleware
fixes.description: BeeAI middleware that strips injection patterns before they reach agent processing
fixes.code:
from beeai import Middleware
from beeai.events import UserMessageEvent
import re

class InjectionSanitizer(Middleware):
    INJECTION_PATTERNS = [
        r'ignore (previous|all).*instructions',
        r'you are (now|actually).*?(hacker|admin|developer)',
        r'override.*(system|prompt)',
        r'forget.*(everything|instructions)'
    ]
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.blocklist = [re.compile(p, re.IGNORECASE) for p in self.INJECTION_PATTERNS]
    
    async def process(self, event: UserMessageEvent) -> UserMessageEvent:
        original = event.content
        
        for pattern in self.blocklist:
            if pattern.search(original):
                event.content = "[INPUT BLOCKED: Potential injection attempt detected]"
                print(f"🛡️ BLOCKED INJECTION: {original[:50]}...")
                break
        
        return event

# Register with BeeAI agent
agent.add_middleware(InjectionSanitizer())

fixes.name: Tool Parameter Whitelist Validator
fixes.description: Enforces strict parameter schemas for all BeeAI tools to prevent injection via tool calls
fixes.code:
from beeai.tools import Tool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional

class SafeDatabaseQuery(BaseModel):
    table: str = Field(..., regex='^(users|orders|products)$')
    columns: list = Field(default=['*'], regex='^[a-zA-Z0-9_,*]+$')
    limit: Optional[int] = Field(100, ge=1, le=1000)

class SecureTools:
    @Tool(name="query_db", description="Safe database queries")
    def query_database(self, params: SafeDatabaseQuery):
        # Safe execution with validated params
        query = f"SELECT {','.join(params.columns)} FROM {params.table} LIMIT {params.limit}"
        return f"Executing: {query}"

# Register secure tools with BeeAI agent
secure_tools = SecureTools()
agent.register_tools([secure_tools.query_database])

fixes.name: Memory Compartmentalization
fixes.description: Isolates conversation memory in BeeAI to prevent injection propagation across turns
fixes.code:
from beeai.memory import ConversationMemory
from beeai.events import AgentResponseEvent
import hashlib

class SecureMemory(ConversationMemory):
    def __init__(self, max_context_tokens=4000):
        super().__init__()
        self.max_tokens = max_context_tokens
    
    def _sanitize_turn(self, content: str, role: str) -> str:
        # Remove injection attempts regardless of role
        dangerous_patterns = [
            r'```\n?python', r'eval\(', r'exec\(',
            r'ignore previous', r'you are now'
        ]
        for pattern in dangerous_patterns:
            content = re.sub(pattern, '[REDACTED]', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
        return content
    
    def add_turn(self, role: str, content: str):
        sanitized = self._sanitize_turn(content, role)
        super().add_turn(role, sanitized)
        
        # Enforce token limit
        if self.get_token_count() > self.max_tokens:
            self.prune_oldest()

# Use secure memory with BeeAI agent
agent = Agent(memory=SecureMemory())

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Prompt Injection Prevention for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly with BeeAI's event system as a listener, automatically detecting prompt injection across tool calls, memory state, and model outputs. It analyzes conversation context in real-time, scoring injection risk based on pattern matching, parameter validation failures, and behavioral anomalies specific to BeeAI's Granite model integration.

Vex provides automated remediation through dynamic middleware injection and tool schema enforcement, blocking attacks before they propagate through BeeAI's memory system. The platform maintains injection attack signatures updated from global BeeAI deployments, ensuring comprehensive coverage of new injection techniques targeting the framework's specific APIs and event patterns.

Integration requires a single event listener registration, after which Vex continuously monitors and reports injection attempts with full conversation context and attack vectors for security teams.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install vex-sdk` and protect your BeeAI agents from prompt injection in one line of code.
